plugins.name: video-bug-analyzer
plugins.description: Diagnose UI and rendering bugs from a screen recording. Give Claude a video and roughly when the bug happens; the plugin extracts frames — an overview contact sheet, scene-cut detection, or per-timestamp zoom with before/after strips — then reasons over them to pinpoint the defect. Also quantifies FPS stutter / dropped frames and freeze gaps (--stutter) and frame-pacing jitter from the real presentation timestamps (--pacing). Strong on persistent visual bugs, honest about flicker, timing, and off-screen state. Requires ffmpeg (auto-installs where the sandbox allows).

plugins.name: repo-bootstrap
plugins.description: Set up a repo to use portka-tools plugins (writes .claude/settings.json + optional CI; --print-only for a classifier-safe manual mode), and optionally install the Portka standard setup: a workflow CLAUDE.md, a git/gh permissions allowlist, and an enforced SemVer version sync bound to the repo's existing version (package.json / pyproject.toml / Cargo.toml / VERSION / README) with a basic test suite — plus a native version-sync test (node:test / unittest) for JS/Python repos.

plugins.name: app-website-evaluator
plugins.description: Evaluate an app or website with a standardized scorecard — each dimension 0-100 + letter grade, a weight-averaged overall, and optional --json. Covers SEO, crawlability, AI-readiness, social/sharing assets, security, performance, and growth (communities to join/submit to, PR wins) — tailored to the site's type and community.

plugins.name: tab-chord-formatter
plugins.description: Format a messy guitar tab or chord sheet into a clean, readable layout for screen, or render a consistent monospace PDF songbook (one or many songs, a target songs-per-page) via headless Chromium. Standardizes [Section] labels, aligns chords over the right lyric syllables, tidies a metadata header (Title/Artist/Capo/Key/Tuning) and 6-line ASCII tab blocks — a deterministic script does the cleanup and rendering, the skill applies judgment to re-align and infer structure.
